text.title: java-spring-boot Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for java-spring-boot as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: @Transactional on a controller method or on a private service method.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Spring AOP proxy cannot intercept controller-layer calls or private methods — the annotation is silently ignored. Transactions never open and reads see uncommitted data or write conflicts.

failure-mode.description: Service returns JPA entity instead of DTO.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exposes schema internals, triggers lazy-load exceptions outside @Transactional, prevents circular-reference handling, and couples the contract to the implementation.

failure-mode.description: Field injection (@Autowired on a field) instead of constructor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hides dependencies, couples to Spring, makes beans hard to test without reflection, and prevents immutability.

failure-mode.description: @OneToMany without @EntityGraph or fetch join used in a list endpoint.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hidden N+1 queries: the list query loads N parent rows, then N separate queries load the associations. Invisible until production load hits. @EntityGraph or fetch joins make the intent explicit.

failure-mode.description: spring.jpa.open-in-view not explicitly set to false.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Boot default is true. Controllers can lazy-load after transaction close, hiding N+1 problems until production. Disabling forces fetch decisions into the service layer where they're visible and testable.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Secrets in code are exfiltrated via version control, log files, container images, and backups. Use environment variables or Spring Cloud Vault.

failure-mode.description: Using @SpringBootTest for every test instead of slicing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: @SpringBootTest loads the full context (5–15 seconds per test). Slicing with @WebMvcTest and @DataJpaTest keeps individual tests under 100ms. Test suite balloons and CI gets gamed via skip.
